summary: Tested all 3 Admin Documents tab bug fixes. All 3 fixes verified working: (1) 'Go to Connected Services' button navigates to integrations tab, (2) WorkDrive connect item is now a <button> and navigates to integrations tab correctly, (3) Upload endpoint errors return 400/502 with clear messages (not 500). Backend: 11/11 tests passed. Frontend: 3/3 fix scenarios verified.

Fix 1 - go_to_connected_services_navigat.details: Clicked 'Go to Connected Services' button in Documents tab. Integrations tab became active (data-state=active). Documents tab became inactive. Confirmed via playwright selector check.

Fix 2a - workdrive_connect_is_button.details: WorkDrive connect element is a <button> tag (verified via tagName.toLowerCase()). Previously was a static div that couldn't handle onClick navigation.

Fix 2b - workdrive_connect_navigates.details: Clicking 'Zoho WorkDrive - Connect →' button navigates to integrations tab (data-state=active). Full navigation confirmed via playwright.

Fix 2c - workdrive_connected_badge.details: WorkDrive connected badge (data-testid=workdrive-connected-badge) correctly NOT shown in DOM when WorkDrive is not connected. Badge only shows in the main documents view (not the empty state), and only when is_validated=true.

Fix 3 - upload_502_not_500.details: Upload endpoint returns clear errors: 400 (WorkDrive not connected) when no WorkDrive configured, 404 (Customer not found) for invalid customer. No 500 internal server errors returned. Sync-folders also returns 400 'WorkDrive is not connected' with clear message. The try-except blocks in documents.py wrap WorkDrive API failures with 502 status codes and descriptive messages.

critical_code_review_comments: documents.py: `platform_super_admin` role is NOT in the is_admin check list (`role in ('admin', 'platform_admin', 'partner_admin', 'partner_super_admin', 'partner_staff')`). This means platform_super_admin is treated as a customer for document operations. This appears intentional (platform admin has no tenant context), but could be confusing. Consider adding a comment to clarify the intent.

documents.py: When WorkDrive IS connected but the API call fails at runtime (expired token, permission error), the correct 502 status is returned. However, this path can only be tested with real Zoho credentials, so the exact 502 error path is code-reviewed (not live-tested).
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 3 Documents tab fixes are working. The admin uses login at /api/auth/login with email=admin@automateaccounts.local/ChangeMe123!. In the UI, use partner code 'automate-accounts' then credentials. WorkDrive is not connected (is_validated=false) so the Documents tab shows the 'not connected' empty state. Platform_super_admin is not in the is_admin check in documents.py (intentional - no tenant context). Test file: /app/backend/tests/test_iter253_documents_fixes.py
